title: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw is an Indian billionaire entrepreneur. She is the executive chairperson and founder of Biocon Limited and Biocon Biologics Limited, a biotechnology company based in Bangalore, India and the former chairperson of Indian Institute of Management, Bangalore.
excerpts: **Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw** (born 23 March 1953) is an Indian billionaire entrepreneur. <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> She is the executive chairperson and founder of Biocon Limited and Biocon Biologics Limited, <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> a biotechnology company based in Bangalore , India <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> and the former chairperson of Indian Institute of Management, Bangalore . <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> In 2014, she was awarded the Othmer Gold Medal for outstanding contributions to the progress of science and chemistry. <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 9 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 10 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 11 ]]()</sup> She was on the *Financial Times* 2011 top 50 women in business list. <sup>[[ 12 ]]()</sup> In 2019, she was listed as the 68th most powerful woman in the world by *Forbes* . <sup>[[ 13 ]]()</sup> She was named EY World Entrepreneur Of The Year 2020. <sup>[[ 14 ]]()</sup>

title: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw - Science History Institute
excerpts:
Section Title: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw > A New Biotech Enterprise
Content:
In 1978, at the suggestion of Auchincloss, Mazumdar-Shaw became managing director for Biocon India, now that country’s leading biotechnology enterprise. From its first home in Mazumdar-Shaw’s garage the industrial enzyme business grew exponentially.
Today Biocon delivers world-class therapeutics to patients around the globe, is India’s largest producer of insulin, and has pioneered the world’s only *Pichia* -based recombinant human insulin, which is now available in over 40 countries. Mazumdar-Shaw is the chairman and managing director of the company.
